deprecated_reason: Merged into stage-noise-filter-v1.json (stage_index: 2). Stage 2 now runs Cheerio product extraction BEFORE noise filtering. See stage-noise-filter-v1.json processing_rules.step_0.
stage_index: N/A - was 1 in old 5-stage arch; conflicts with stage-classify-v1.json (stage_index: 1) in new 7-stage arch
name: Structural Parse
purpose: DEPRECATED. Extract product blocks from HTML using Cheerio with location tracking.
design_intent: Deterministic HTML parsing that identifies WHERE products appear, not WHAT they are. Enables downstream stages to filter and validate.
change_impact: Logic ported into Stage 2 Filter (stage-noise-filter-v1.json) as step_0. extractStructure() function preserved but called internally by filterNoise().

required_fields: rawProducts - Array<{ name, price?, duration?, text, location }>

block_detection:
Find

and

elements with price/duration patterns
Track element path as location identifier
product_extraction: Match duration pattern: N개월 or N년
Extract surrounding text as product context

noise_handling.strategy: Extract ALL blocks, let Stage 2 filter
noise_handling.rationale: Stage 1 is purely structural; filtering decisions belong to Stage 2

implementation.reuse: Port logic from parsePpomppuPost() extractProducts() function

test_cases.name: Image-heavy ad
test_cases.input: HTML with images, no text prices

test_cases.name: SharePlan noise
test_cases.expected: rawProducts includes license line, blockLocations shows noise blocks

test_cases.name: Mixed content
test_cases.expected: rawProducts includes ALL blocks (Stage 2 will filter)

acceptance_criteria: blockLocations includes element path for each block
Test cases TC-1.1 through TC-1.4 pass
